release_body:
* Instapush support (@giuseongit)
* Slack support (@juanpabloaj)

* MacOS & shell-integration bugfixes (@eykd)

* Use robots.txt (issue #1)
* Detect and mitigate recursion loops (issue #23)
* Improved installation process compatibility (issue #35)
* Python 3.7 compatibility (issue #34)
* Option `--not-allow-redirects` (issue #15)
* Option `--not-follow-subdomains` (issue #16)
* Option `--max-depth` to follow link (issue #17)

Most important changes:

- Added MIB *status*, *product release* and *revision description* set calls at pysnmp code generator
- Changed REVISION field format in JSON representation - it is now a list of dicts each with *revision* timestamp and *description* text
- MIB REFERENCE fields are only exported if --with-mib-text is on
- Multiple fixes to pysnmp codegen not to produce function calls with more than 255 parameters

Full change log can be seen [here](https://github.com/etingof/pysmi/blob/master/CHANGES.rst).
General bugfixes. README updates.

pip-review now employs [entry points](https://chriswarrick.com/blog/2014/09/15/python-apps-the-right-way-entry_points-and-scripts/). As a consequence, the `pip-review` command now works under Windows and can alternatively be called as `python -m pip_review`. See the [HISTORY](https://github.com/jgonggrijp/pip-review/blob/0.5/HISTORY.md) for further details.

[Read the release notes](http://django-machina.readthedocs.io/en/stable/release_notes/v0.2.0.html)

* Use ``os.fsencode`` and ``os.fsdecode()`` for filepath encoding/decoding in Python 3
* Do not decode the 'name' field of events in Python 2, and only encode unicode strings passed to ``add_watch()``, not strings that are already bytestrings.

These two changes allow ``inotify_simple`` to handle filepaths that might not respect the filesystem encoding. In Python 2, leaving everything as bytes mean we don't care about encoding at all. In Python 3, using ``os.fsencode()`` and ``os.fsdecode()``, which use the ``surrogateescape`` error handler, allows incorrectly encoded filepaths to make the decode/encode round-trip unchanged. All standard library functions that encode or decode filepaths do so in this way, so filepaths returned by a standard library calls can be passed to ``add_watch()``, with correct behaviour even if the filepath was incorrectly encoded on the filesystem.

- Add an 'after date' condition #15

Deis v1.7.1 fixes an issue with deis-logger publishing keys to `etcd` too frequently, causing high CPU load. All Deis users are encouraged to upgrade to this release.

### v1.7.1 -> v1.7.2

#### Fixes

- [`c73c8f7`](https://github.com/deis/deis/commit/c73c8f7e0fc96576f90a6cd4c2a66d08ba2dbae9) logger: adjust publish and ttl times to seconds

The module has been published to PyPI.

View HISTORY.rst of the module for a changelog.

https://pypi.org/project/azure-cli-iot/0.1.9

Full release notes at https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/release-notes-azure-cli
- Upgraded to *Cliquet* 2.7.0

Minor bugfix:
- kipoi model source is now using https instead of ssh. fixes https://github.com/kipoi/kipoi/issues/211
- sort all models alphabetically before testing with `kipoi test-source ...`
Added python 3 compatibility

* Add a client for making Rpc calls to apps built with [Mobly Snippet Library](https://github.com/google/mobly-snippet-lib).
* Add a controller lib for attenuators.
* Add customizable log prefix tag in AndroidDevice for better device-level logging.

This is a bugfix release of RobotPy, and all RobotPy users are recommended to upgrade.

* The pynetworktables dependency has been updated to 2015.1.0, which fixes a number of problems that could cause your robot to freeze when using NetworkTables. See https://github.com/robotpy/pynetworktables/pull/11 for more information.
* Minor simulator related clarified error messages

See all changes on [github](https://github.com/robotpy/robotpy-wpilib/compare/2015.0.7...2015.0.8).

* Add ability to remove repository location (#86)
* Fixed bug in connection parsing when federated connections are present (#87)
* Fixed bug in UNICODE support (#80)

* Performance focused release, including:
* Enable Shapely speedups, when available
* Skip inners which cause exceptions
* Union inners in blocks when making valid
* Make benchmark script python3 compatible
* Fix test to support different versions of GEOS

* Return more detailed errors when validation fails
* Validate "date-time" Swagger format

This release adds some client-side field validation:
- IDFA, IDFV, and AAID must all be valid/parseable as UUID's
- the custom_attributes property of `AppEvent`,` ScreenViewEvent`, and `CommerceEvent` must be str, int, bool, or float. They cannot be lists.

The SDK will throw a `ValueError` if any of the cases above are violated.
Find 0.1.0 in changelog for more details

Release 1.5.2 was committed and tagged on 2018-03-30

- Bugfix: Allow null and empty condition names.
- Other: Added compatibility tests using Django 1.11.

The library was already compatible with Django 1.11, without a code change, so we were lucky. But now we have tests to prove it and eventually get an alert if there's a regression.
Added Hotfix from #308: Extract conditions and filter them using the fields that exist in the form.
Fixed a regression whereby `--keep-overlaps`, `--keep-direction` and `--conversion-error` options were being ignored instead of passed on to the ufo2ft compiler (7eb44b48).

[BUGFIX] Handle non-ASCII characters in /proc/pid/stat
[BUGFIX] Make check for Python 2.6 work on development versions of Python

Added the ability to set URL parameters via the Api instance:
- Api.setUrlParameter(name, value), Note: a single URL parameter may be removed by setting value = None
- Api.getUrlParameter(name)
- Api.clearUrlParameters()

Example: Api.setUrlParameter('output', 'rosette') will append ?output=rosette to the endpoint URL and return additional information (subject to change).

Note: Once set on an Api instance, the parameter will remain active for all endpoint calls.
